title: Introduction
body: Welcome to Zyplo documentation.
Zyplo is a developer-focused project management tool designed to help teams plan faster and ship better.
In this guide, you’ll learn how to set up your workspace, manage projects, and track tasks efficiently.

title: Getting Started
body: Create an account and log in to Zyplo.
Create your first workspace for your team.
Create a project inside the workspace.
Invite your teammates by email.
Create and assign your first task.

subsections.title: What is a Workspace?
body: A workspace is your team’s main space in Zyplo.
It contains all your projects, members, and settings.
You can create multiple workspaces for different teams or companies.

subsections.title: What is a Project?
body: A project represents a product or codebase.
Each project lives inside a workspace.
Projects contain tasks, boards, and activity history.

subsections.title: What is a Task?
body: A task is a unit of work.
Each task has a title, description, status, priority, assignee, and due date.
Tasks can be moved across the board as work progresses.

subsections.title: Task Status Workflow
body: Zyplo uses a simple workflow: To Do → In Progress → Review → Done.
You can move tasks between these states using drag and drop.
This helps the team understand what is being worked on right now.

subsections.title: What is a Board?
body: The board is a visual representation of your project.
It shows tasks grouped by their current status.
Each column represents a step in your workflow.

subsections.title: What are Columns?
body: Columns represent different stages like To Do, In Progress, Review, and Done.
You can move tasks between columns to update their status.
This makes progress visible to the whole team.

subsections.title: What are Cards?
body: Each task is shown as a card on the board.
Cards contain task details like title, assignee, priority, and due date.
Clicking a card opens the full task view.

title: Command Palette
body: Press Ctrl + K to open the command palette.
You can quickly jump to projects, create tasks, assign issues, and change status or priority.
This helps you navigate Zyplo without leaving the keyboard.

title: Teams & Roles
body: Admins can manage projects and members.
Members can create, edit, and move tasks.
Roles help keep your workspace organized and secure.

title: Notifications & Activity
body: You’ll receive notifications when a task is assigned to you.
You’ll also be notified when status changes or someone comments.
The activity log shows who did what and when.

title: Shortcuts & Tips
body: Ctrl + K — Open command palette.
Use labels to group tasks.
Use priorities to plan sprints.
Check the dashboard daily to stay updated.
